title: 309A Practice Exam 1 — Red Seal Aligned
description: 150 questions aligned to Red Seal NOA weightings. Moderate difficulty with balanced question types covering all exam domains.

questions.question: A split-phase induction motor uses a start winding that is disconnected by a centrifugal switch at approximately what speed?
questions.explanation: The centrifugal switch opens at approximately 75% of synchronous speed, disconnecting the start winding once the motor has accelerated.

questions.question: On a single-line (one-line) electrical diagram, a circle with an X through it represents:
questions.explanation: A circle with an X is the standard symbol for a ceiling-mounted light fixture on electrical floor plans.

questions.question: At what sling angle does the working load limit decrease significantly?
questions.explanation: Below 45 degrees the load on each leg increases significantly.

questions.question: A paint spray booth is classified as what hazardous zone per the CEC?
questions.explanation: The interior of a spray booth is classified as Zone 1 — an area where flammable gas/vapour is likely to exist under normal operating conditions.

questions.question: First action when a co-worker is in contact with an energized source?
options: A) Pull them away by clothing
D) De-energize or use non-conductive object to separate them
questions.explanation: Remove the hazard first.

questions.question: Incident energy of 8.5 cal/cm2 requires what minimum PPE category?
questions.explanation: Category 2 (8 cal/cm2) is insufficient; Category 3 required.

questions.question: Fire extinguisher class required near electrical equipment?
questions.explanation: Class C is for energized electrical fires.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Job Safety Analysis (JSA)?
options: C) Identify hazards and safe procedures for each step
questions.explanation: JSA identifies step-by-step hazards and controls.

questions.question: Minimum clearance for a scaffold near 44 kV overhead lines?
questions.explanation: 6 m for 750V-150 kV.

questions.question: A warehouse requires 300 lux at floor level. Each LED fixture provides 18,000 lumens with a coefficient of utilization of 0.65 and a maintenance factor of 0.80. Approximately how many fixtures are needed for a 500 m2 area?
questions.explanation: Fixtures = (lux x area) / (lumens x CU x MF) = (300 x 500) / (18000 x 0.65 x 0.80) = 150,000 / 9,360 = ~16 fixtures.

questions.question: When is an energized work permit required?
options: D) When de-energizing is infeasible or creates greater hazard
questions.explanation: When de-energizing is infeasible or creates greater hazard.

questions.question: The limited approach boundary is:
options: B) Shock hazard distance for unqualified persons
questions.explanation: Shock hazard boundary for unqualified persons.

questions.question: An electric baseboard heater rated at 2000W, 240V is installed on a dedicated circuit. The minimum conductor and breaker size is:
questions.explanation: 2000W / 240V = 8.33A. As continuous load: 8.33 x 1.25 = 10.4A. #12 AWG on 20A breaker handles this with margin.

questions.question: Which is NOT a LOTO requirement?
options: B) Using same lock for multiple workers
questions.explanation: Each worker needs their own lock.

questions.question: Who provides Safety Data Sheets?
questions.explanation: Manufacturer/supplier provides SDSs.

questions.question: GFCIs on construction sites trip at:
questions.explanation: 5 mA for personnel protection.

questions.question: Maximum 90-degree bends in a conduit run between pull points?
questions.explanation: 360 total degrees = four 90-degree bends.

questions.question: Minimum burial depth for NMWU 120/240V without mechanical protection?
questions.explanation: 600 mm for 0-750V direct buried.

questions.question: Standard residential wiring cable in Canada?
questions.explanation: NMD90 is standard for residential.

questions.question: 100A feeder, RW90 copper, 6 conductors in raceway, 40C ambient. Minimum conductor size?
questions.explanation: Derating factors: 0.80 x 0.88 = 0.704. #1 AWG at 90C = 145A x 0.704 = 102A > 100A.

questions.question: Minimum copper conductor size for branch circuits?
questions.explanation: #14 AWG minimum for branch circuits.

questions.question: AC90 armoured cable is NOT permitted in:
questions.explanation: Not permitted in Zone 0 or Zone 1 hazardous locations.

questions.question: Maximum conduit fill for 3+ conductors?
questions.explanation: 40% fill for 3 or more conductors.

questions.question: How must NMD90 be supported along joists?
questions.explanation: Secured every 1.5 m and within 300 mm of boxes.

questions.question: Minimum bonding conductor for a 200A service to metal water pipe?
questions.explanation: #6 AWG copper per Table 16.

questions.question: Voltage drop on 120V, #12 AWG Cu, 30 m run, 16A load?
questions.explanation: Using CEC method: Vd = 2 x 30 x 16 x R/km = ~6.4V (5.3%).

questions.question: CEC specifies a standard receptacle mounting height?
options: D) The CEC does not specify a standard height
questions.explanation: No mandated height; 300 mm is convention only.

questions.question: Box fill for a device box depends on:
options: C) Box volume and all conductors, clamps, devices, and grounds
questions.explanation: Must account for all conductors, clamps, devices, and grounds.

questions.question: Minimum headroom in front of an electrical panel?
questions.explanation: 2.0 m minimum headroom.

questions.question: Neutral conductor colour in Canada?
questions.explanation: White or grey for identified (neutral) conductor.

questions.question: TECK90 cable through a fire-rated wall requires:
options: A) Nothing - armour provides fire rating
B) Approved firestop maintaining the fire-resistance rating
C) Fire-resistant tape 300 mm each side
D) MI cable only through fire walls
questions.explanation: All fire-rated penetrations need approved firestop systems.

questions.question: Sub-panel in detached garage - neutral and ground must be:
options: A) Bonded together at the sub-panel
B) On separate bus bars at both ends
C) Connected through a GFCI breaker
D) Kept separate - bonded only at main service panel
questions.explanation: Neutral and ground separate in sub-panels.

questions.question: Maximum flexible cord length for a kitchen range?
questions.explanation: 1.8 m (6 ft) maximum.

questions.question: Underground parking block heater receptacles must be:
options: C) GFCI-protected with max 8 per circuit
D) Single receptacles on individual 20A circuits
questions.explanation: GFCI-protected with limited receptacles per circuit.

questions.question: Minimum clearance from 120/240V service mast to openable window?
questions.explanation: 1.0 m minimum clearance.

questions.question: NMD90 entering a metal box requires:
options: D) No connector if knockout fits
questions.explanation: Approved cable connector at every metal box entry.

questions.question: Maximum wall distance from a receptacle in a residential living room?
options: A) 1.8 m from any point along the wall
questions.explanation: No point along the wall more than 1.8 m from a receptacle.

questions.question: Conduit fill: 4x#10 THHN, 2x#6 THHN, 1x#10 bare ground. Minimum EMT size?
questions.explanation: Total area fits 40% fill of 27 mm EMT.

questions.question: Minimum device box depth?
questions.explanation: 38 mm (1-1/2 in) minimum.

questions.question: TECK90 cable is primarily used for:
questions.explanation: Industrial/commercial applications needing mechanical protection.

questions.question: EMT through a concrete block wall - protect conductors with:
questions.explanation: Bushing or fitting protects against conductor abrasion.

questions.question: Maximum overcurrent protection for #14 AWG copper branch circuit?
questions.explanation: 15A maximum for #14 AWG.

questions.question: Hospital 100A copper feeder - minimum equipment bonding conductor?
questions.explanation: #6 AWG per Table 16 for 100A.

questions.question: PVC conduit support intervals depend on:
options: D) The trade size of the conduit
questions.explanation: Support intervals vary by conduit diameter.

questions.question: Bonding bushing is required when conduit terminates at:
questions.explanation: Required at concentric/eccentric knockouts.

questions.question: Grounding conductor colour?
options: D) Green or green with yellow stripe
questions.explanation: Green or green/yellow for grounding.

questions.question: 400A service with parallel conductors - minimum grounding electrode conductor?
questions.explanation: #2/0 AWG Cu per Table 17 for 400A.

questions.question: Minimum clear working space in front of 600V panelboard?
questions.explanation: 1000 mm (1 m) minimum.

questions.question: In a 120/208V 3-phase wye system, phase-to-phase voltage is:
EMT is NOT permitted where:
options: D) Subject to severe physical damage
questions.explanation: EMT not allowed where subject to severe physical damage.

questions.question: Minimum free conductor length at each outlet box?
questions.explanation: 300 mm from where conductor emerges.

questions.question: Kitchen split receptacle minimum conductor size?
options: D) #12 AWG on shared neutral MWBC
questions.explanation: #12 AWG on 20A multi-wire branch circuit.

questions.question: A 600V feeder in an aluminum raceway requires what bonding method?
options: B) Double locknuts on each end
C) Bonding bushings or bonding-type locknuts at each end
D) No special bonding - aluminum conducts
questions.explanation: Bonding bushings ensure reliable bonding at aluminum raceways.

questions.question: What is the standard voltage for a 3-phase service in a Canadian commercial building?
questions.explanation: 347/600V is standard Canadian commercial 3-phase.

questions.question: A dwelling unit has 180 m2. What is the basic lighting load?
questions.explanation: 180 m2 x 30 W/m2 (CEC Table 14) = 5,400W basic lighting load.

questions.question: What is the minimum insulation temperature rating for conductors in a dry location raceway?
questions.explanation: 60 degrees C is the minimum standard rating for dry locations.

questions.question: GFCI trips immediately when reset. Most likely cause?
options: A) Ground fault on load-side wiring
D) Neutral and ground properly separated
questions.explanation: Ground fault on downstream wiring.

questions.question: 3-phase motor hums but won't start. Voltage on only 2 of 3 phases. Cause?
options: A) Single-phasing (blown fuse/tripped breaker on one phase)
questions.explanation: Single-phasing - one fuse blown or breaker tripped.

questions.question: Megger test on 600V motor reads 0.5 MOhm. This means:
options: C) Marginal - investigate moisture or contamination
questions.explanation: Minimum for 600V motor is ~1.6 MOhm. 0.5 is well below.

questions.question: Flickering lights that worsen when appliances cycle on indicate:
options: D) Loose/high-resistance connection at service entrance
questions.explanation: High-resistance connection causes voltage fluctuations.

questions.question: Thermal image shows one fuse hotter than others in balanced 3-phase disconnect. Indicates:
options: D) High-resistance connection or deteriorating fuse
questions.explanation: Uneven heating = high resistance at that connection.

questions.question: Breaker trips under load but current is below rating. Likely cause?
questions.explanation: Defective breaker with weakened thermal element.

questions.question: MCC motor starter has nuisance tripping with correct overload sizing. Check FIRST:
options: B) Voltage imbalance between phases at starter
questions.explanation: Small voltage imbalance causes large current imbalance.

questions.question: Receptacle tester shows 'open ground' means:
questions.explanation: Grounding conductor not properly connected.

questions.question: Surge protector shows fault after lightning strike; devices damaged. Explanation:
options: B) Lightning can't damage indoor equipment
D) SPD MOVs were exceeded by surge magnitude
questions.explanation: Surge exceeded SPD capacity, allowing damaging voltage through.

questions.question: Continuity test on conduit run shows high resistance. Issue:
questions.explanation: Loose/corroded coupling breaks bonding path.

questions.question: Tenant feels tingling at kitchen faucet. 4V AC measured to ground. Cause:
options: D) Failed bonding between water piping and grounding system
questions.explanation: Broken equipotential bonding allows current on piping.

questions.question: Best instrument for measuring current without disconnecting wire?
questions.explanation: Clamp meter senses magnetic field around conductor.

questions.question: Overheating neutral bus with multi-wire branch circuits suggests:
options: A) Shared neutrals carrying harmonic currents exceeding capacity
questions.explanation: Triplen harmonics add on neutral instead of cancelling.

questions.question: LED buzzing on dimmer. Best fix:
options: B) Install dimmer rated for LED loads and verify compatibility
questions.explanation: LED-compatible dimmer resolves buzzing.

questions.question: AFCI trips intermittently, no visible arcing. Possible cause:
options: A) Nail/screw has pierced cable insulation in wall
questions.explanation: Concealed cable damage from nail/screw creates intermittent arc.

questions.question: First step troubleshooting a dead receptacle?
options: B) Open wall to inspect wiring
C) Check if circuit breaker tripped
questions.explanation: Start at the source - check breaker first.

questions.question: 208V motor draws high current on one phase with balanced voltage. Suggests:
questions.explanation: Balanced voltage but unbalanced current = motor winding issue.

questions.question: Emergency lights fail during outage but batteries are full. Cause:
options: B) LED emergency lights don't work in outages
C) Transfer switch/relay that detects power loss has failed
questions.explanation: Sensing circuit or transfer relay has failed.

questions.question: A warm circuit breaker during normal operation is:
options: A) Normal due to internal resistance
D) Too many wires in panel
questions.explanation: Moderate warmth is normal from current flow.

questions.question: 3-phase contactor chatters. Coil voltage reads low. Cause:
options: D) Low control voltage from excessive drop or failing transformer
questions.explanation: Low coil voltage prevents full armature engagement.

questions.question: DMM shows 0.5-3V AC between ground wire and metal pipe. Indicates:
options: D) Stray voltage or neutral-ground fault
questions.explanation: Fluctuating AC on piping warrants ground fault investigation.

questions.question: Commissioning: 600V switchgear shows ground fault alarm, no loads, insulation reads 200 MOhm. Cause:
options: A) Contamination on ground fault sensor/CT
questions.explanation: False alarm from contamination on GF sensor - bus insulation is fine.

questions.question: Programmable thermostat loses program after power outage. Check:
questions.explanation: Dead backup battery causes program loss.

questions.question: VFD 'overcurrent' fault on startup, motor runs fine across-the-line. Check:
options: C) VFD parameters - acceleration time and motor data
questions.explanation: VFD acceleration time too short or motor data entered wrong.

questions.question: What tool identifies which breaker controls a specific receptacle?
questions.explanation: Circuit tracer sends signal from receptacle to identify breaker.

questions.question: Bus duct joint temps increase over months at constant load. Indicates:
options: A) Progressive oxidation and increasing contact resistance
questions.explanation: Progressive resistance increase from oxidation/loosening.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a contactor in a motor circuit?
options: B) Switch motor on/off remotely via control signal
questions.explanation: Contactor switches motor power using lower-voltage control.

questions.question: DOL starter suitability depends on:
options: B) Available supply capacity relative to motor locked-rotor current
questions.explanation: Supply stiffness vs inrush current matters, not just HP.

questions.question: Wye-delta starter reduces starting current to approximately:
questions.explanation: 1/3 of DOL current.

questions.question: Thermal overload relay protects against:
questions.explanation: Protects motor from sustained overcurrent/overheating.

questions.question: 30 HP, 575V, 3-phase motor (FLC 32A). Maximum dual-element time-delay fuse?
questions.explanation: 175% x 32A = 56A. Round DOWN to standard 50A.

questions.question: Swapping two phases of a 3-phase motor causes:
options: B) Draws more current same direction
questions.explanation: Reversing two phases reverses rotation.

questions.question: PLC input LED on but program doesn't see it. Cause:
options: B) Wrong input address in program
questions.explanation: Address mismatch between physical terminal and program.

questions.question: Short circuit protection in a motor starter is provided by:
questions.explanation: Fuses/breaker handle short circuits.

questions.question: VFD motor overheats at 30 Hz reduced load. Why?
options: C) Shaft fan provides less cooling at low speed - need external blower
D) VFDs always overheat below 50 Hz
questions.explanation: TEFC fan airflow decreases with speed.

questions.question: Seal-in contact in a 3-wire start/stop circuit is:
options: B) NO auxiliary on contactor in parallel with start button
questions.explanation: NO auxiliary parallels start button to hold circuit.

questions.question: Purpose of a control power transformer (CPT)?
options: A) Step down 600V to 120V for control circuits
questions.explanation: CPT steps down line voltage for control devices.

questions.question: FLC on a motor nameplate stands for:
questions.explanation: Full Load Current at rated conditions.

questions.question: Soft starter stalls centrifugal pump on 15-second ramp. Fix:
options: A) Increase initial torque (kick start) and/or ramp time
questions.explanation: Pump needs more breakaway torque - increase kick start.

questions.question: Forward/reverse interlocks prevent:
options: B) Simultaneous energization causing short circuit
questions.explanation: Electrical and mechanical interlocks prevent both contactors closing.

questions.question: PLC output LED on but solenoid valve doesn't actuate. Check:
options: C) Output fuse, field wiring, and valve coil
questions.explanation: Program is working; problem is downstream.

questions.question: VFD faults on 'output overcurrent' with 60 m cable. Output reactor fixes it because:
options: B) Limits dV/dt from PWM and cable capacitance reducing reflected wave spikes
questions.explanation: Long cables + PWM = reflected waves. Reactor limits dV/dt.

questions.question: 'OL' on a motor schematic represents:
questions.explanation: OL = overload relay.

questions.question: Disconnect switch for a single motor must be rated at minimum:
questions.explanation: 115% of motor FLC minimum.

questions.question: 50 HP, 575V motor, Code G. Expected locked-rotor current?
questions.explanation: Code G = 5.6-6.29 kVA/HP. Calculated LRA approximately 395A.

questions.question: E-stop button should use what contact type?
questions.explanation: NC contacts = fail-safe. Wire break also stops machine.

questions.question: Minimum separation between power and unshielded comm cables?
questions.explanation: 200 mm prevents EMI.

questions.question: Cat6 UTP maximum channel length?
options: A) 90 m permanent link, 100 m channel
questions.explanation: 90 m permanent + 10 m patches = 100 m channel.

questions.question: Maximum untwisted Cat6 conductor length at termination?
questions.explanation: 13 mm max to maintain performance.

questions.question: Single-mode fiber optic cable specification?
questions.explanation: 9/125 um is single-mode.

questions.question: 48 data drops need a telecom room. Minimum floor space per TIA?
questions.explanation: ~10 m2 minimum per TIA-569.

questions.question: Standard commercial Cat6 wiring scheme?
options: C) T568A one end, T568B other
questions.explanation: T568B most common in North America.

questions.question: Primary advantage of fiber over copper?
options: A) EMI immunity and longer distance
questions.explanation: Fiber is immune to EMI, supports longer runs.

questions.question: PoE++ (Type 3/4) uses how many conductor pairs?
questions.explanation: PoE++ uses all 4 pairs for up to 90W.

questions.question: Cat6 fails NEXT at patch panel, passes everything else. Fix:
options: D) Re-terminate with minimal pair untwisting
questions.explanation: NEXT at one end = termination issue.

questions.question: Plenum space comm cable must be rated:
questions.explanation: CMP rated for fire safety in plenum spaces.

questions.question: Minimum Cat6 bend radius during installation?
questions.explanation: 4 times cable diameter.

questions.question: WAP needs 30W PoE at 85 m. Minimum standard and cable?
questions.explanation: 30W needs PoE+. At 85 m, Cat6 has lower resistance.

questions.question: Fire alarm installation standard in Canada?
questions.explanation: ULC-S524 governs FA installation.

questions.question: Fire alarm circuit wire colour in Canada?
questions.explanation: Red insulation for fire alarm circuits.

questions.question: 2-stage fire alarm: Stage 1 activates ___, Stage 2 activates ___:
options: C) Alert signal, then general evacuation alarm
D) Strobes only, then audible alarms
questions.explanation: Stage 1 = alert (investigate), Stage 2 = evacuate.

questions.question: Fire alarm system testing frequency?
questions.explanation: Annual verification plus monthly inspections.

questions.question: NAC circuit max resistance is determined by:
options: A) Total device current draw and minimum operating voltage at last device
questions.explanation: Calculated from total load and voltage drop to last device.

questions.question: Required initiating device at stairwell entrances in high-rises?
questions.explanation: Pull stations at every stairwell entrance.

questions.question: Fire alarm wiring must be:
options: A) Kept separate from all other wiring and clearly identified
B) In same raceway as power wiring
C) Always in rigid metal conduit
questions.explanation: Separate and identified from other wiring.

questions.question: Fire alarm annunciator panel location:
options: A) Main entrance accessible to fire services
B) Mechanical room near fire pump
questions.explanation: At main entrance for firefighter access.

questions.question: Photoelectric smoke detector uses:
options: A) Light source and photoelectric sensor
questions.explanation: Light source scattered by smoke onto sensor.

questions.question: Sprinkler flow switch is classified as:
questions.explanation: Flow switch = water flowing = fire = alarm initiation.

questions.question: Purpose of end-of-line (EOL) resistor?
options: C) Supervise circuit for open/ground faults via reference current
questions.explanation: EOL provides reference current for fault supervision.

questions.question: Emergency lighting minimum illumination duration?
questions.explanation: 30 minutes minimum.

questions.question: CEC section covering solar PV systems?
questions.explanation: Section 64 covers Renewable Energy.

questions.question: PV conductors must be sized at:
options: D) Load current at main panel
questions.explanation: 125% of short-circuit current.

questions.question: Grid-tied inverter must have:
questions.explanation: Anti-islanding disconnects when grid is lost.

questions.question: 12 PV modules in series, 40V Voc each, temp correction 1.14. Max system voltage?
CEC section for EV charging (EVSE)?
questions.explanation: Section 86 covers EVSE.

questions.question: Level 2 EV charger supply:
questions.explanation: 208V or 240V dedicated circuit.

questions.question: Battery energy storage system (BESS) requires:
options: B) Disconnect, overcurrent protection, ventilation, and signage
questions.explanation: Full protection package per CEC.

questions.question: PV rapid shutdown must reduce array conductors to ___ within 30 seconds:
questions.explanation: 80V or less for first responder safety.

questions.question: Megohmmeter purpose:
questions.explanation: Tests insulation resistance in megohms.

questions.question: New 600V switchgear - minimum insulation resistance reading?
questions.explanation: 100 MOhm+ expected for new equipment.

questions.question: Ground resistance test reads 12 ohm for building electrode:
options: A) Exceeds typical limit - supplemental electrodes may be needed
questions.explanation: 10 ohm is typical max; 12 ohm needs improvement.

questions.question: Phase rotation testing ensures:
options: A) Correct A-B-C sequence for proper motor rotation
questions.explanation: Confirms phase sequence for motor direction.

questions.question: Transformer turns ratio test shows 0.8% deviation. This is:
options: D) Within tolerance per IEEE C57.12 depending on ratio
questions.explanation: Tolerance depends on transformer ratio per IEEE C57.12.

questions.question: Test that verifies breaker trips at rated current:
questions.explanation: Primary injection applies actual current to verify trip.

questions.question: Ground resistance target 5 ohm, first test reads 8 ohm. Best approach:
options: B) Add rods at 2x rod length spacing and retest
C) Drive existing rods deeper only
D) Use water pipe ground only
questions.explanation: Supplemental rods at proper spacing maximizes reduction.

questions.question: Before energizing a new installation in Ontario, you need:
questions.explanation: ESA must inspect and approve.

questions.question: CEC stands for:
questions.explanation: Canadian Electrical Code, CSA C22.1.

questions.question: AHJ for electrical installations in Ontario?
questions.explanation: ESA is Ontario's AHJ.

questions.question: All electrical equipment in Canada must be:
options: C) Approved with certification mark from accredited body
questions.explanation: Must bear CSA, cUL, cETL or equivalent certification mark.

questions.question: Current CEC edition referenced by Ontario (2024)?
questions.explanation: 26th Edition, CSA C22.1:24.

questions.question: In the CEC, 'shall' means:
questions.explanation: 'Shall' = mandatory. 'Should' = recommended. 'May' = permitted.

questions.question: The CEC applies to:
options: B) All electrical installations and equipment in buildings and premises
questions.explanation: All installations including modifications and additions.

questions.question: Special ESA inspection is required for:
options: D) Temporary wiring, emergency systems, or code deviations
questions.explanation: Special inspections for life safety, temporary, hazardous, variances.

questions.question: If CEC doesn't address a specific method, you should:
options: A) Use any method you choose
B) Consult the AHJ (ESA) for guidance
questions.explanation: Consult AHJ for guidance and approval.
